message:
demo_showcase: add loading state components and fix async cancel test

New loading module (loading.rs):
- LoadingSpinner: animated spinner with configurable styles (Dot, Line,
  Pulse, Points, MiniDot) and optional label text
- SkeletonLine/SkeletonBlock: placeholder components for content loading
- LoadingOverlay: centered overlay for modal loading states
- PulsingIndicator: subtle pulsing dot indicator
- All components respect accessibility: --no-animations flag and
  REDUCE_MOTION environment variable disable animation ticks and show
  static fallback indicators instead

Async runner test fix:
- cancel_where_removes_matching_operations test was previously ineffective
  because sync fallback mode completes operations immediately (pending
  always empty)
- Test now manually inserts PendingOperation entries into runner.pending
  HashMap to properly verify cancel_where removes only matching operations
  while preserving non-matching ones (LoadDocsIndex survives while both
  FetchMetrics operations are cancelled)

Snapshot updates:
- Updated jobs view snapshots for both terminal sizes
- Removed stale .snap.new comparison files after accepting changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1bja","title":"charmed_log: Handle RwLock poisoning gracefully instead of panic","description":"# Bug: RwLock Poisoning Causes Application-Wide Crash

## Problem Statement
The charmed_log crate uses .unwrap() on all RwLock read/write acquisitions.
If any thread panics while holding the lock, the lock becomes "poisoned" and
ALL future lock acquisitions will panic, cascading to crash the entire application.

## Affected Code Locations
```
Line 427: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 468: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 475: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 481: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 488: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 494: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 500: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 506: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 512: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 518: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
Line 528: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 579: let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();
Line 597: let mut inner = self.inner.write().unwrap();
```

## Root Cause Analysis
The pattern `self.inner.read().unwrap()` assumes the lock will never be poisoned.
However, if a thread panics during:
- Format string processing (e.g., invalid UTF-8)
- Style rendering (e.g., lipgloss issue)
- Any code path holding the lock

Then ALL subsequent logging calls crash with:
`called Result::unwrap() on an Err value: PoisonError { ... }`

## Solution Design
Replace all .unwrap() with .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) pattern:

```rust
// Before (panics on poisoned lock):
let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap();

// After (recovers from poisoning):
let inner = self.inner.read().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
```

This pattern:
1. Recovers the inner data even if lock was poisoned
2. Allows logging to continue functioning
3. Is the recommended pattern for "must not fail" operations like logging

## Alternative Considered: Return Result
Changing the API to return Result<(), LogError> was considered but rejected because:
- Breaking API change
- Logging should be fire-and-forget
- Callers shouldn't need to handle logging errors in their business logic

## Implementation Steps
1. Create helper macro or function to encapsulate the unwrap_or_else pattern
2. Replace all 13 occurrences
3. Add test that verifies logging continues after simulated panic
4. Document the recovery behavior

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn logging_continues_after_panic_in_another_thread() {
    let logger = Logger::new();
    
    // Spawn thread that panics while holding lock
    let logger_clone = logger.clone();
    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        // Acquire lock and panic
        // (requires test-only hook to simulate)
    }).join().ok();
    
    // This should NOT panic - logging should continue
    logger.info("After panic", &[]);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 .unwrap() calls replaced with poison-recovery pattern
- [ ] Test demonstrates continued operation after poisoning
- [ ] No performance regression (unwrap_or_else is zero-cost when lock is healthy)
- [ ] Documentation updated to note recovery behavior","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:34.303042431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:49.131050035Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:49.130977760Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - all RwLock accesses use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) pattern","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","critical","panic-fix","thread-safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bja","depends_on_id":"bd-3ju1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:34.329172028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1cgb","title":"EPIC: E2E Test Coverage with Detailed Logging","description":"End-to-end tests for complete application workflows with comprehensive logging.

## Philosophy
- Test full user workflows, not just units
- Detailed logging for debugging test failures
- Capture terminal state at each step
- Support both headless and interactive modes

## Target Applications
1. demo_showcase - primary E2E target
   - All 8 pages (Dashboard, Services, Jobs, Logs, Docs, Files, Wizard, Settings)
   - Navigation flows (sidebar, keyboard shortcuts)
   - Theme switching and persistence
   - Shell-out and restore patterns
   - Wizard deployment workflow

2. glow - markdown viewer
   - File loading and rendering
   - Navigation and search
   - Stash operations

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp every test step
- Log all key presses and expected outcomes
- Capture full terminal state on failure
- Include ANSI sequence dumps for debugging
- Log timing information for performance tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.027535577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:14:46.025801319Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:14:46.025720748Z","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: logging framework (bd-28kk), shell-out tests (bd-3ilg), wizard workflow (bd-3nit), navigation flows (bd-2zj3), logs filtering (bd-1s7t), theme/settings (bd-21sm). E2E Test Coverage epic complete.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.034668586Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E LOGGING REQUIREMENTS - Non-Negotiable:

Every E2E test MUST include detailed logging that makes debugging failures possible
without re-running the test interactively. This is critical for CI/CD reliability.

REQUIRED LOG CONTENT:
1. Test identification (name, timestamp, environment)
2. Each test step with description
3. All input events (key presses, mouse clicks)
4. Expected outcome for each assertion
5. Actual outcome (pass/fail with details)
6. Terminal state capture on failure
7. Timing information for performance analysis

LOG FORMAT EXAMPLE:
```
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.123Z] TEST_START: test_wizard_deployment_flow
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.124Z] ENV: TERM=xterm-256color, COLORTERM=truecolor
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.125Z] STEP: Initialize application
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.130Z] STEP: Navigate to Wizard page (key: '7')
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.131Z] INPUT: KeyEvent { code: Char('7'), modifiers: NONE }
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.135Z] ASSERT: current_page == Page::Wizard
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.135Z] RESULT: PASS
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.136Z] STEP: Enter service name 'test-service'
...
```

The infrastructure task (bd-28kk) MUST be completed first - all other E2E tests
depend on it for consistent logging.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:55Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dr6","title":"demo_showcase page: Jobs/Tasks (async workflows, progress, filtering)","description":"A realistic CI/job-runner screen: a dense jobs list/table with progress, filtering, and actions that feel like a real ops console.

Why:
- This page is the best “integration crucible” for the stack:
  - bubbletea message routing + command composition
  - bubbles components (table/list, progress, spinner, paginator, timer/stopwatch)
  - harmonica motion for compelling progress/counter animation (optional)
  - charmed_log/toasts for outcomes and errors
- It proves the architecture scales beyond toy demos.

Must showcase (minimum):
- Navigation + selection:
  - keyboard-first (arrows/jk, enter), consistent focus model
  - selection stability across refreshes and filter changes
- Status + progress:
  - determinate progress bars and indeterminate spinners
  - color-coded statuses (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
  - durations + ETA-ish hints (timer/stopwatch)
- Realistic operations:
  - start/cancel/retry actions implemented via Cmd/AsyncCmd
  - action results visible (toast + log + UI state)
- Discoverability:
  - query bar + filtering + sorting
  - paginator for large lists
- Details pane:
  - shows job metadata, last logs, parameters, timeline / steps

Data requirements:
- Driven by the deterministic simulation engine and seeded generator.
- Integrates with Wizard: wizard triggers new jobs; jobs correlate to logs.

Acceptance:
- Feels like an actual CI dashboard, not a toy.
- Actions are responsive: input never blocks, state updates are obvious.
- Filtering is fast and predictable.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filtering/sorting correctness and stability
  - action state machine (start/cancel/retry) transitions
  - progress rendering decisions (spinner vs bar, colors)
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - smoke tour coverage
  - optional “jobs filtering” scenario if needed for regression.

Notes:
- Keep the information density high, but never sacrifice clarity.
- Make sure `--no-animations` still looks good (no “blank” experience).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.395269743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:41.424387508Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dr6","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.423951863Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1fxb","title":"Logs: filtering + search (level/service/query)","description":"Add filtering + search controls to the Logs page.

Why:
- Logs are only useful when you can slice them quickly.
- This is also a great way to showcase bubbles textinput + the app’s focus/keybinding model.

Filters to implement:
- Level selector (e.g., TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
- Service selector (from demo domain model)
- Free-text query (substring or simple matcher)

Performance requirements:
- Filtering should feel instant.
- Avoid re-rendering/parsing the entire log history on every keystroke.
- Prefer an incremental or pre-indexed filter model that can be unit tested.

UX/polish:
- Highlight matches in the viewport (optional).
- Bracketed paste should work in the query input.
- Show an inline summary: "123 lines (12 matches)".

Acceptance:
- Filtering feels instant and scales to a large buffer.
- Covered by unit tests at the filtering-engine layer.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.782877999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352469043Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352332649Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.799160009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:41.631552397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:39.044747386Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:55:00.660400674Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:55:00.660271694Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.434141459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:59.432221685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:00.706143825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g80","title":"E2E scenario: file picker navigate + preview","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that exercises the Files page (bubbles FilePicker + preview).

Scenario outline:
1) Launch demo_showcase in deterministic headless mode.
2) Navigate to Files.
3) Use the file picker to:
   - move selection
   - enter a directory (if supported)
   - select a file
4) Validate preview behavior:
   - preview pane becomes non-empty
   - breadcrumb / filename reflects the selected entry
   - selecting a markdown fixture renders via glamour (not raw markdown), using the current theme

Notes:
- Avoid relying on the host filesystem. Use the demo’s deterministic fixture directory (from the asset strategy / Config).

Acceptance:
- Deterministic in CI.
- Failure artifacts include the directory listing, selected filename, and the last rendered Files frame.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.191594688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:06.564367389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:06.564301366Z","close_reason":"Integrated Files page into app (Page enum, Pages struct, routing). Added E2E tests for file picker navigation and preview functionality. Tests verify: fixture mode, j/k navigation, Enter to select/enter, Backspace to go back, and hidden file toggle.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","files","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-1wtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.230688217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-22c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:58.608108725Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.221867959Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.217633407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.226320857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.213212187Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2zj3","title":"E2E Tests: demo_showcase navigation flows","description":"End-to-end tests for demo_showcase navigation and page flows.

## Purpose
Test complete user workflows through demo_showcase,
verifying the app works as a real user would experience it.

## Test Requirements

### Application Startup
- [ ] App starts and renders initial dashboard
- [ ] Sidebar shows all pages
- [ ] Status bar displays correctly
- [ ] Help overlay accessible via '?'

### Page Navigation
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts 1-8 navigate pages
- [ ] Sidebar selection works
- [ ] Page state persists during navigation
- [ ] Back navigation if implemented

### Each Page Smoke Test
- [ ] Dashboard - metrics load and display
- [ ] Services - service list renders
- [ ] Jobs - job list with progress
- [ ] Logs - log entries display, filter works
- [ ] Docs - documentation tree navigable
- [ ] Files - file browser works
- [ ] Wizard - can start wizard flow
- [ ] Settings - settings toggle correctly

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log every key press with timestamp
- [ ] Log expected vs actual page after nav
- [ ] Capture terminal state on failure
- [ ] Include timing information

## Success Criteria
- All navigation paths tested
- Each page renders without error
- No dead-end states","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldPeak","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:27.855435034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:47:24.353840429Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:47:24.353771681Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:27.878940499Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:03.377359642Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zj3","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:38.911195389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zkx","title":"Integration Tests: Cross-platform terminal support","description":"Integration tests for cross-platform terminal compatibility.

## Purpose
Ensure charmed_rust works correctly across different
terminals and platforms.

## Test Requirements

### Terminal Capability Detection
- [ ] Color support detection (16/256/RGB)
- [ ] Unicode support detection
- [ ] Mouse support detection
- [ ] Alternate screen support

### Graceful Degradation
- [ ] ASCII-only fallback mode
- [ ] 16-color fallback
- [ ] No-mouse fallback
- [ ] Dumb terminal handling

### Platform-Specific
- [ ] Linux terminal emulators
- [ ] macOS Terminal.app
- [ ] Windows Terminal
- [ ] Windows ConHost (legacy)
- [ ] SSH sessions
- [ ] tmux/screen

### Environment Variables
- [ ] TERM handling
- [ ] COLORTERM handling
- [ ] NO_COLOR support
- [ ] FORCE_COLOR support
- [ ] CI detection

## Test Approach
Use environment variable mocking to simulate
different terminal environments.

## Success Criteria
- Works in all major terminals
- Degrades gracefully when features unavailable
- Respects user terminal preferences","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldPeak","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:14.596526172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:57.615013775Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:23:57.614885346Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-platform","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zkx","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:14.612515273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-30md","title":"Animations: page transitions + focus highlight","description":"Add high-impact motion polish: page transitions + focus highlight.

Why:
- Smooth transitions are a huge part of the "turned up to 11" feel.
- This also demonstrates harmonica-based animation integrated into bubbletea’s update loop.

What to animate:
- Page transition:
  - subtle slide/fade or wipe between pages (Dashboard → Jobs → Docs, etc.)
  - transition should be fast and never feel sluggish
- Focus highlight:
  - clearly indicate which pane is focused (sidebar vs main vs modal)
  - when focus changes, animate the highlight rather than snapping (unless reduce-motion)

Constraints:
- Must respect `--no-animations` / reduce-motion:
  - animations become instant state changes
- Must never cause layout corruption:
  - transitions should not change measured widths/heights mid-frame

Testability:
- Animation invariants are covered by the animation unit tests.
- Key UI snapshots should include at least one focused vs unfocused view.

Acceptance:
- Transitions feel premium and consistent.

+{"id":"bd-30md","title":"Animations: page transitions + focus highlight","description":"Add high-impact motion polish: page transitions + focus highlight.

Why:
- Smooth transitions are a huge part of the "turned up to 11" feel.
- This also demonstrates harmonica-based animation integrated into bubbletea’s update loop.

What to animate:
- Page transition:
  - subtle slide/fade or wipe between pages (Dashboard → Jobs → Docs, etc.)
  - transition should be fast and never feel sluggish
- Focus highlight:
  - clearly indicate which pane is focused (sidebar vs main vs modal)
  - when focus changes, animate the highlight rather than snapping (unless reduce-motion)

Constraints:
- Must respect `--no-animations` / reduce-motion:
  - animations become instant state changes
- Must never cause layout corruption:
  - transitions should not change measured widths/heights mid-frame

Testability:
- Animation invariants are covered by the animation unit tests.
- Key UI snapshots should include at least one focused vs unfocused view.

Acceptance:
- Transitions feel premium and consistent.
- Reduce-motion mode still looks good (no broken visuals).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:56.791266325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:08:40.186900226Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","polish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:56.825831852Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:11.824345470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-30md","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:10.604501900Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32pp","title":"Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log","description":"Implement the *styled* log formatting layer for the Logs page using `charmed_log`.

Why:
- A flagship ops-style demo lives and dies by log readability.
- This showcases that `charmed_log` + lipgloss styling can produce beautiful, information-dense output.

Formatting requirements:
- Each log entry should include (where available):
  - timestamp (compact)
  - level (TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
  - service/component name
  - message
- Styling:
  - color-coded levels (warn/error pop, info muted)
  - consistent column alignment and truncation
  - preserve legibility in narrow terminals
- No-color mode:
  - degrade gracefully (no ANSI), still aligned and readable

Integration:
- Logs are produced by the demo domain simulation and by user actions.
- The formatted line should carry the structured fields needed for filtering (level/service/query) without re-parsing the rendered string.

Acceptance:
- Logs look great, are readable at a glance, and remain usable with thousands of lines.
- Deterministic formatting (important for snapshot/E2E artifacts).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:37.574456901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:16.801798652Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:16.801717932Z","close_reason":"Implemented LogFormatter with styled, aligned output supporting color-coded levels, no-color mode, and configurable column widths. Added 8 tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:37.594269536Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-33fe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:28.585526827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-32pp","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:31.153270873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33fe","title":"demo_showcase data: structured log stream + formatting","description":"Create the underlying structured log stream used by the demo (domain simulation + user actions).

Why:
- The Logs page should not be "just strings". We want structured fields so filtering/search is fast and correct.
- It proves the integration story: domain layer emits events, UI renders + filters them.

Deliverables:
- A `LogEntry` type (or similar) with fields like:
  - timestamp / monotonic tick
  - level
  - service/component
  - message
  - optional correlation ids (job id, deployment id)
- A log buffer/stream:
  - append-only collection with a bounded retention policy (optional)
  - API to push new entries from simulation/actions

Integration:
- The Logs viewer uses this stream and a formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`) to render.
- Filtering operates on structured fields, not re-parsed rendered text.

Acceptance:
- Large log volumes remain responsive.
- Entries support correlation (wizard → job → logs) and filtering by level/service.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:53.990998123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336843390Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336748333Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured log stream with filtering. Enhanced LogEntry with tick, job_id, deployment_id correlation. Created LogStream with filtering by level/target/job/deployment/text/tick-range, count_by_level(), and bounded retention. Added 16 new tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:54.007146912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:48.231233898Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-39my","title":"demo_showcase data: async job runner demo (AsyncCmd)","description":"When the `bubbletea/async` feature is enabled, demonstrate a realistic async workload pipeline using `AsyncCmd` (non-blocking background work that produces messages).

Why:
- Async is a headline capability: it proves TUIs can do real I/O work without blocking input.
- The showcase should prove both *correctness* (no races/panics) and *ergonomics* (clean update logic).

What to demonstrate (in-app):
- I/O-like operations:
  - “fetch metrics”, “deploy service”, “load docs index”, “export logs”, etc.
  - each operation returns a message that updates state + triggers UI feedback
- Concurrency:
  - multiple AsyncCmds can run concurrently (batch)
  - results reconcile safely into the model
- Cancellation semantics (best-effort):
  - bubbletea AsyncCmd itself doesn’t inherently cancel tasks; demonstrate cancellation via:
    - per-job generation IDs / cancellation tokens
    - ignoring late results for canceled jobs
    - UI shows “canceled” and stops progress updates

Implementation notes:
- Feature-gate the async runner with `cfg(feature = "async")` and provide a sync fallback path so the demo still works without async.
- Prefer deterministic delays in test mode (e.g., fixed durations or seeded RNG) so E2E is stable.

Acceptance:
- Async mode feels like a real app doing background work while remaining responsive.
- No input blocking; no terminal corruption; no runaway task spawning.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - async pipeline updates state correctly (use tokio test + deterministic time where feasible)
  - cancellation token/generation logic ignores late results
  - batch/sequence composition behaves as expected
- E2E:
  - at least one scenario runs with async enabled (can be a separate CI job if needed) and asserts job progression + completion.

Notes:
- Keep the API surface small: most of the complexity should live in demo_showcase domain state, not in glue.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyGlen","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:52.895189964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:59:00.694234598Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:52.911117280Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:53.253293494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:51.982805847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39te","title":"Unit tests: println/printf gating (only when no-alt-screen)","description":"Add unit tests ensuring the demo_showcase uses bubbletea `println/printf` intentionally and only in the right mode.

Why:
- `println/printf` is a powerful feature but can easily create messy output if used in alt-screen.
- We want a crisp, deterministic policy: only emit unmanaged lines when the user explicitly opts in.

Test coverage:
- When Config indicates alt-screen is enabled (default):
  - events that would normally log/notify do *not* produce `PrintLineMsg` commands.
- When Config indicates `--no-alt-screen`:
  - the same events do produce `bubbletea::println/printf` output (i.e., `PrintLineMsg`).

Implementation hint:
- Keep the policy in a small helper (e.g., `maybe_print_line(config, msg) -> Cmd`) so tests can execute the Cmd and assert on the returned Message type.

Acceptance:
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a real terminal.
- Failures clearly indicate which mode produced unexpected output.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.095304097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:14.125758892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:14.125658425Z","close_reason":"Unit tests for println/printf gating complete. Created tests/println_gating_tests.rs with 9 tests covering deployment event command production, bubbletea runtime gating documentation, command execution, and config alt_screen settings. All tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.120238122Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.110807003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-7iul","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.115944759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-3aio","title":"Jobs: timer/stopwatch for durations + ETA","description":"Add realistic time semantics to Jobs: durations, ETAs, and timers.

Why:
- CI/ops dashboards live and die by good time cues ("how long", "how much left").
- This showcases `bubbles::stopwatch`/`bubbles::timer` in a product context.

What to show:
- Per-job:
  - start time
  - elapsed duration (stopwatch)
  - ETA / remaining time (timer or derived estimate)
- Global:
  - an overall "pipeline" timer (optional)

Integration:
- Use bubbletea tick/every for updates (no wall-clock sleeps).
- Tie to job state transitions:
  - stop timers when job completes
  - freeze time when job is paused/cancelled

Acceptance:
- Time displays are stable, readable, and update smoothly.
- No flakiness in tests (no dependence on real time).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:23.055080227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:50:24.298668057Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","timer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3aio","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:23.075813532Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3aio","depends_on_id":"bd-3m25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:05.033421770Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3aio","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:07.636146806Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3b7o","title":"Settings: keybindings reference + customization hooks","description":"Display a comprehensive keybindings reference inside Settings (and optionally link to it from the help overlay).

Why:
- The demo must be self-teaching. Users should never feel stuck or need to read source.
- A single canonical keymap also prevents "random per-page keys" that make TUIs feel amateurish.

What to show:
- Global keys (quit, help, back/close, focus next/prev, search/command palette, theme switch, guided tour).
- Page-specific shortcuts (Dashboard drill-down, Jobs start/cancel, Logs follow, Docs search, Files open/preview).
- Interaction conventions:
  - list/table navigation
  - viewport scrolling
  - form submit/cancel

Nice-to-have:
- Show the *effective* keymap (reflecting feature flags/toggles):
  - if command palette not built, omit it
  - if mouse disabled, hide mouse tips

Acceptance:
- The key reference is readable, nicely styled, and matches the actual behavior.
- The app feels self-teaching: new users can explore without guessing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:05.551180539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:46:54.870194089Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:46:54.870127965Z","close_reason":"Implemented keybindings reference section in Settings page.  {"id":"bd-xn0u","title":"Integration Tests: huh + bubbletea forms","description":"Integration tests for huh forms in bubbletea TUI context.

## Purpose
Verify huh form components work correctly within the
bubbletea event loop and command system.

## Test Requirements

### Form in TUI
- [ ] Form receives keyboard events
- [ ] Focus management works
- [ ] Validation messages display
- [ ] Form submission triggers commands

### Field Components
- [ ] TextInput in form context
- [ ] Select dropdown rendering
- [ ] MultiSelect toggle behavior
- [ ] Custom field implementations

### Navigation
- [ ] Tab moves between fields
- [ ] Enter submits or advances
- [ ] Escape cancels appropriately
- [ ] Mouse clicks focus fields

### Async Validation
- [ ] Validators can return commands
- [ ] Loading states display
- [ ] Error handling works

## Test Approach
Use bubbletea simulator with huh forms.
Simulate user completing form workflow.

## Success Criteria
- Forms feel responsive in TUI
- All field types integrate correctly
- No event handling bugs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:00.874473841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:30.076997112Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","huh","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xn0u","depends_on_id":"bd-1u1s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:30.076965463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xn0u","depends_on_id":"bd-2q8k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:28.606303878Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xn0u","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:00.909265759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-y3c8","title":"demo_showcase data: background simulation engine (tick/every)","description":"Implement a background simulation that updates data over time.

Use:
- bubbletea tick/every commands (and async equivalents if enabled) to drive state changes.

Testability requirement (critical):
- The simulation must be drivable *without real sleeps*.
  - Prefer a `Tick` message that carries `Instant`/frame count.
  - In production, schedule ticks with `tick/every`.
  - In unit/e2e tests, inject `Tick` messages directly (ProgramSimulator / custom IO) to advance time deterministically.

Behaviors:
- Jobs progress and complete/fail.
- New log lines appear.
- Service health flaps occasionally.
- Deployments start/finish.

Acceptance:
- UI feels alive and realistic without being chaotic; changes drive visible UI updates.

-{"id":"bd-yghg","title":"Animations: loading states + micro-interactions","description":"Add micro-interactions and loading states that make the demo feel alive and premium.

Why:
- Small motion + state feedback is what separates a toy TUI from a "real product".
- This also stress-tests the animation subsystem and tick scheduling.

Examples to implement (pick a few that fit the UI):
- Loading spinners + skeleton/placeholder content for:
  - Docs loading
  - Jobs list refresh
  - File preview loading
- Subtle animations:
  - toast slide-in/out
  - progress easing
  - pulsing warning indicator for degraded service

Constraints:
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - replace motion with instant state changes and clear static indicators
- Must not increase CPU usage unreasonably.

Acceptance:
- Loading states feel intentional and beautiful.
- No-animations mode still communicates state clearly.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:59.479257020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:02:45.736930092Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","spinner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:59.534704063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:08.114572604Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:09.336208750Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ygt5","title":"Property Tests: bubbles component state machines","description":"Property-based tests for bubbles component state invariants.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify state machine invariants hold
for all possible input sequences.

## Properties to Test

### Viewport
- [ ] scroll_position always in [0, max_scroll]
- [ ] visible_lines <= content_lines
- [ ] viewport_top + visible_lines <= content_lines

### Progress
- [ ] percentage always in [0.0, 1.0]
- [ ] set_percent clamps out-of-range values
- [ ] width never negative

### TextInput
- [ ] cursor_position in [0, content_length]
- [ ] cursor never beyond content
- [ ] delete at position 0 is no-op
- [ ] insert increases length by 1

### Spinner
- [ ] frame_index always in [0, frame_count)
- [ ] tick advances by 1 (mod frame_count)
- [ ] custom frames work same as built-in

### List
- [ ] selected_index in [0, items.len()) or None
- [ ] visible_items <= items.len()
- [ ] filter matches subset of items

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn viewport_scroll_stays_bounded(
        content_lines in 1usize..1000,
        viewport_height in 1usize..100,
        scroll_ops in prop::collection::vec(0i32..10, 0..50),
    ) {
        let mut vp = Viewport::new(content_lines, viewport_height);
        for op in scroll_ops {
            vp.scroll(op);
            assert!(vp.scroll_position() <= vp.max_scroll());
        }
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- State invariants always hold
- No panic for any input sequence
- Edge cases (empty, max) handled","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.619524500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683687463Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683652437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.653068222Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yqh4","title":"demo_showcase: flagship 'turned up to 11' showcase binary","description":"Create a new standalone Rust binary crate (`crates/demo_showcase`) that acts as the *flagship* demo app for the whole charmed_rust ecosystem.

Why:
- Prove end-to-end functionality across crates in one realistic, cohesive application.
- Provide a visually stunning, highly polished reference implementation ("how to build real apps with this stack").
- Serve as a regression surface: if the demo stays amazing, the library is healthy.

What this demo must showcase (integrated, not toy snippets):
- bubbletea: model/update/view architecture, commands, timers, resize handling, mouse events, focus, paste, async mode (feature-gated), graceful exit/terminal restore.
- lipgloss: layout (join/place), borders, width/height, alignment, wrapping, truncation, theming + live theme switching.
- bubbles: multiple components working together (viewport, list, table, progress, spinner, textinput/textarea, filepicker, paginator, timer/stopwatch, help).
- glamour: markdown rendering with themes + syntax highlighting (when enabled), width-aware wrapping, link formatting.
- huh: real form flows (validation, multi-step, confirm/select) embedded as an in-app workflow.
- wish (optional runtime mode): run the same app over SSH (or a subset) to prove SSH viability.
- charmed_log: styled logging output and/or an in-app log viewer.
- harmonica: animations/transitions (numbers, progress easing, panel transitions).

Definition of done (high level):
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` launches a coherent app with multiple pages/panels and a beautiful theme.
- Every major crate feature above is exercised in a way that looks like a real-world product.
- Documented run instructions + keybindings; no panics; terminal state restored on exit.

Notes:
- Prefer a single cohesive "product" story (e.g., ops console / project dashboard) over a grab-bag.
- Use a strong theming system so everything feels unified.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:55.126209979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:55.148432813Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","flagship","showcase"]}

- Tone (professional, crisp), icon usage (if any), and spacing/punctuation conventions.
- Error/warning copy patterns (actionable, short).

Acceptance:
- Avoids lorem ipsum feel; content supports the "ops console" illusion.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:08.017050344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:40:36.920166282Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:40:36.920039195Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["content","demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zc72","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:03.542206253Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-zc72","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:08.042123242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
